cmd/jobs/billing.purpose: Cobra parent command aggregating four billing sub-commands (advance, advancecharges, collect, subscriptionsync) under the 'billing' namespace of the jobs CLI. A pure aggregator — no business logic; all service access flows through internal.App fields wired by app/common.

patterns.name: Aggregator-only parent command
patterns.description: billing.go registers sub-commands in init() and exposes a single Cmd var — no RunE, no flags, no logic.

patterns.name: Sub-commands in separate packages
patterns.description: Each sub-command (advance, advancecharges, collect, subscriptionsync) lives in its own sub-package imported and registered in billing.go's init().

patterns.name: internal.App singleton for service access
patterns.description: Sub-commands access billing services only through internal.App fields (BillingAutoAdvancer, ChargesAutoAdvancer, BillingSubscriptionReconciler) — never construct services locally.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Every RunE uses cmd.Context() — never context.Background()/TODO().

patterns.name: Nil guard for optional features
patterns.description: advancecharges guards every execution path against app.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil because charges are wired conditionally (credits.enabled).
patterns.example: if app.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil { return errors.New("charges feature disabled") }

patterns.name: Flags on the owning sub-command, not the parent
patterns.description: Shared filter flags like --namespace belong on the owning sub-command (PersistentFlags), never duplicated across siblings or pushed to the billing parent Cmd.

patterns.name: Structured input types from domain packages
patterns.description: collect and subscriptionsync use typed input structs (billingworkercollect, reconciler) rather than loose primitives to track service API evolution.

key_file_guides.role: Pure aggregator — registers the four sub-commands in init() and exposes Cmd for the parent jobs command.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add RunE, flags, or business logic here; this file must remain a zero-logic aggregator.

key_file_guides.role: List, advance-single, and advance-all sub-commands via BillingAutoAdvancer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses a nil slice (not empty string) for the optional namespace filter — do not default to empty string.

key_file_guides.role: Charge advance sub-commands; nil-guards ChargesAutoAdvancer before every call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Omitting the nil guard panics when the charges feature is disabled (credits.enabled=false).

key_file_guides.role: Invoice collection sub-commands using billingworkercollect structured input types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always supply time.Now() for CollectionAt — passing nil is a known debt in InvoiceCmd; do not replicate it. Use StringSliceVar for multi-value filters.

key_file_guides.role: Subscription-to-invoice sync reconciliation via BillingSubscriptionReconciler — the manual recovery path for missed billing-worker Kafka events.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always apply a lookback filter; omitting it scans all historical subscriptions. AllCmd needs --force to bypass sync-state guards.

anti_patterns: Constructing billing or charges service instances locally instead of using internal.App fields
Using context.Background()/TODO() instead of cmd.Context() in any RunE
Registering flags on the billing parent Cmd that belong only to a specific sub-command (flag pollution)
Hardcoding namespace strings instead of accepting them via a --namespace flag
Omitting the nil guard on app.ChargesAutoAdvancer — panics when the charges feature is disabled

decisions.decision: billing.go is a pure aggregator with no logic
decisions.rationale: Keeps sub-command concerns isolated so each evolves independently without touching siblings.

decisions.decision: All service access goes through internal.App
decisions.rationale: Prevents duplicate wiring and ensures the same Wire-provisioned instances (with correct feature flags) are used everywhere.

decisions.decision: Each sub-command lives in its own package
decisions.rationale: Allows independent flag registration, testability, and import without pulling in sibling Cobra state.

cmd/jobs/ledger.purpose: Cobra parent command grouping ledger administrative sub-commands (currently only backfillaccounts). Owns the ledger namespace in the jobs CLI and coordinates sub-packages that intentionally bypass Wire DI to construct concrete ledger adapters, because the wired account resolver is a noop when credits.enabled=false.

patterns.name: Aggregator-only parent command
patterns.description: ledger.go registers sub-commands in init() and exposes Cmd — no RunE, no flags, no logic.

patterns.name: Bypass DI for the concrete ledger stack
patterns.description: Sub-commands build concrete ledger adapters directly (not via app/common Wire outputs) because the Wire-provided resolver is a noop when credits.enabled=false.

patterns.name: Flags in init(), logic in RunE
patterns.description: All flag bindings go in init(); RunE contains all execution logic including time-flag parsing.

patterns.name: RFC3339 time parsing with UTC normalization
patterns.description: Time-valued flags are parsed from RFC3339 strings inside RunE and explicitly normalized to UTC.
patterns.example: t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, flagVal); t = t.UTC()

patterns.name: Print summary before returning error
patterns.description: Backfill commands print a result summary (processed/failed counts) before returning any error so partial results stay visible to operators.

patterns.name: Interface-driven service in a separate package
patterns.description: Backfill orchestration lives in a separate service sub-package with interface-driven deps, enabling unit testing without a real DB or Cobra context.

patterns.name: Cursor pagination with MAX_SAFE_ITER guard
patterns.description: Paginated loops in the service package use paginationv2.Cursor and enforce MAX_SAFE_ITER to prevent infinite loops on large customer sets.
patterns.example: for iter := 0; cursor != nil && iter < paginationv2.MAX_SAFE_ITER; iter++ { ... }

key_file_guides.role: Pure aggregator — registers backfillaccounts.Cmd in init() and exposes Cmd.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add RunE, flags, or business logic to ledger.go.

key_file_guides.role: Cobra command: flag binding in init(), concrete adapter construction, and RunE execution.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must build lockr.NewLocker — accountservice.New requires a Locker for CreateCustomerAccounts. Do not use the Wire-provided ledger resolver (noop when credits.enabled=false).

key_file_guides.role: Pure Go backfill orchestration with no Cobra/Ent imports; interface-driven for unit testability.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Detect missing-account via ValidationIssue error codes, not nil checks. DryRun must only increment WouldProvision, never write.

key_file_guides.role: Ent-backed CustomerLister implementation used by the service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must use cursor pagination, not offset, to handle large customer sets safely.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests using mock CustomerLister and account service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use t.Context(), not context.Background(), so cancellation is tied to the test harness.

anti_patterns: Using the Wire-provided ledger account resolver from app/common — it is a noop when credits.enabled=false
Omitting lockr.NewLocker when constructing the account service — panics at CreateCustomerAccounts
Using offset pagination instead of cursor pagination in large customer loops
Returning an error from RunE before calling printSummary — partial results become invisible to operators
Importing *entdb.Client directly in service.go — breaks unit testability

decisions.decision: Build concrete ledger adapters directly instead of using DI outputs
decisions.rationale: app/common wires ledger services to noops when credits.enabled=false; backfill jobs need real ledger writes, so they construct the concrete stack themselves.

decisions.decision: Separate service package from the Cobra command
decisions.rationale: Isolates orchestration from CLI plumbing, enabling unit tests without a real DB or Cobra context.

decisions.decision: Detect missing-account via ValidationIssue error codes, not nil checks
decisions.rationale: Ledger adapters return typed ValidationIssue errors for missing accounts; nil-checking Get results is unreliable and breaks backfill idempotency.
